chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Virtue of Month of Ramadan and the Reward of Fasting in it

text: 1836 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ لِجَابِرٍ : «يَا جَابِرُ مَنْ دَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ فَصَامَ نَهَارَهُ وَ قَامَ وِرْداً مِنْ لَيْلِهِ وَ حَفِظَ فَرْجَهُ وَ لِسَانَهُ وَ غَضَّ بَصَرَهُ وَ كَفَّ أَذَاهُ خَرَجَ مِنَ اَلذُّنُوبِ كَيَوْمَ وَلَدَتْهُ أُمُّهُ» قَالَ جَابِرٌ قُلْتُ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ مَا أَحْسَنَ هَذَا مِنْ حَدِيثٍ قَالَ «مَا أَشَدَّ هَذَا مِنْ شَرْطٍ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1836 - Abu Ja‘far (Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as)) said to Jabir:
"O Jabir, whoever reaches the Month of Ramadan, fasts its days, stands in worship for part of its nights, guards their chastity and tongue, lowers their gaze, and refrains from harming others will emerge from their sins as on the day their mother gave birth to them."
Jabir said: "I replied, 'May I be your ransom, how beautiful this narration is!'
Imam (as) said: 'How stringent its conditions are!'"
